original_hypothesis.title: MerkleClaimable.setConfig() Can Invalidate Active Claims by Changing Merkle Root
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The owner can call setConfig() at any time to change merkleRoot, claimStart, and claimEnd without constraints. Changing the merkle root mid-claim period invalidates all unclaimed entries from the previous tree. Users who had valid proofs become unable to claim. The owner could also set claimStart > claimEnd or merkleRoot to bytes32(0), which triggers the ongoingClaim modifier to revert for all claims. Already-claimed entries remain marked (isClaimed[hash] = true), but these hashes are tied to the old root's leaf structure.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: For ERC20Airdrop, ERC20Airdrop2, and ERC721Airdrop contracts, the merkle root change can permanently lock out eligible users from their airdrop allocations. Combined with the deployment evidence showing init with merkleRoot = bytes32(0), there's a temporal gap where claims are impossible until owner sets a valid config.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployERC20Airdrop.s.sol initializes with claimStart=0, claimEnd=0, merkleRoot=bytes32(0) — claims impossible until owner calls setConfig

proof_of_signal.pattern: setConfig(uint64 _claimStart, uint64 _claimEnd, bytes32 _merkleRoot) external onlyOwner — no validation that new root preserves unclaimed entries; no constraint on timing relative to active claims

preconditions: Active claim period with unclaimed entries
Owner calls setConfig with different merkleRoot
Users have proofs for old root but not new root

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: New merkleRoot invalidates all old proofs immediately
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner changes root to exclude specific claimants
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Excluded users lose their airdrop allocation permanently

economic_questions: Is setConfig protected by timelock/multisig in deployment?
Can owner rug unclaimed airdrop allocations?
What is the total unclaimed value at any point?
validation_requirements: Test: set config with new root during active claims, verify old proofs fail
Check if deployment script implements any safety measures

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% of unclaimed tokens retained by protocol

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check governance controls on owner address

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
